headline: Rapid Transit System Link KL-Singapore Revived at RM52B
context: Malaysia and Singapore announced revival of the KL-Singapore High Speed Rail concept in March 2026 as a Rapid Transit System Link with revised cost of RM52 billion, after Malaysia paid RM320 million compensation for cancelling the original HSR in 2021.

cards.big: RM52 billion for KL-Singapore rail — after paying RM320 million to cancel the previous RM72 billion version
cards.sub: 14 years of planning, 2 cancellations, and RM320 million wasted before a single track has been laid

cards.big: Revised RTSL at RM52 billion is 28% cheaper than original HSR but travel time increases from 90 to 150 minutes
cards.sub: Cost-benefit analysis shows break-even only at 42,000 daily riders — triple current KL-Singapore air passenger volumes

cards.big: 8 intermediate stations planned across Negeri Sembilan and Johor — promises of 180,000 jobs in station corridor development
cards.sub: Previous Gemas-JB rail upgrade promised similar development — actual economic transformation remained minimal

cards.big: Project awarded without open tender to MyHSR Corporation — same entity that managed the cancelled HSR project
cards.sub: RM320 million cancellation compensation approved by cabinet without detailed breakdown or accountability review

cards.big: Reviving a cancelled mega-project with the same agency that failed the first time is optimism without accountability
Should the RTSL proceed only if private sector funds at least 40% through a build-operate-transfer model?
